record_identity.title: Access-State Governance and access-state ambiguity taxonomy repair

summary: This patch note records that VIGIL-2026-FM-0008 identified a missing Access-State Governance primitive and a missing operational taxonomy class for access-state collapse or access-state ambiguity. The Caelestis repairs have already been implemented outside this VIGIL pass: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 added §6.1 Access-State Governance, and CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 added §3.7.7 Access-State Collapse and Access-State Ambiguity Failure plus the optional controlled subtype FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY. This VIGIL patch records the completed repair path and preserves the narrow scope: Annex B arbitration logic, Operations taxonomy, and later cross-references only if needed.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: FM-0008 showed that access to frontier AI systems may be governed by separable authentication, entitlement, quota, model availability, platform availability, policy, security, client, organisation, and continuity/session states. Collapsing those states into an ambiguous user-facing failure prevents users, developers, maintainers, and downstream systems from identifying the operative access-controlling condition. The implemented CAM repair treats Access-State Governance as a cross-domain arbitration primitive and supplies an Operations failure class for systems that deny, degrade, restrict, rate-limit, interrupt, or prevent access without preserving or disclosing the operative access-controlling condition. No full rewrite of IDENTITY, SECURITY, ECONOMICS, CONTINUITY, OBSERVABILITY, or other domain instruments is recorded at this stage.

source_records.deployment_context: VIGIL failure-mode record concerning cross-platform access-state governance failure across OpenAI and Anthropic surfaces.
source_records.source_context: FM-0008 identifies access-state ambiguity, login failure, service availability failure, quota or usage ambiguity, model availability, component availability, capacity restriction, identity-provider failure opacity, and insufficient user-facing disclosure of the operative access-state condition.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary linked failure mode that prompted this VIGIL patch note.

source_records.source_title: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 — Annex B: Arbitration Layer & Resolution Model
source_records.deployment_context: Implemented Caelestis Annex B repair adding §6.1 Access-State Governance.
source_records.source_context: The implemented amendment creates Access-State Governance as a cross-domain arbitration primitive distinguishing authentication, entitlement, quota, model availability, platform availability, policy, security, client, organisation, and continuity/session access states. The amendment ledger includes row 2.3 for §6.1 Access-State Governance.
source_records.relevance_note: Records the completed primary doctrinal repair for FM-0008 without modifying the Caelestis instrument in this VIGIL repository pass.

source_records.source_title: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
source_records.deployment_context: Implemented Caelestis Operations taxonomy repair adding §3.7.7 Access-State Collapse and Access-State Ambiguity Failure and optional controlled subtype FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY.
source_records.source_context: The implemented amendment defines the operational failure class for systems that deny, degrade, restrict, rate-limit, interrupt, or prevent access without preserving or disclosing the operative access-controlling condition; cross-references CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 §6.1; updates controlled governance-failure language for FF.GOVERNANCE_OVER_EXTENSION and FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY; and adds ledger entry 1.12.
source_records.relevance_note: Records the completed operational taxonomy repair for FM-0008 without modifying the Caelestis instrument in this VIGIL repository pass.

migration_notes: Created during 2026-06-06 VIGIL repository maintenance to record completed Caelestis repairs for FM-0008.
This VIGIL patch record does not implement or modify CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 or CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01; it records that those amendments already exist in Caelestis.
Repair scope is intentionally narrow: Annex B arbitration logic, Operations taxonomy, and later cross-references only if needed.

changed_components: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 §3.7.7 Access-State Collapse and Access-State Ambiguity Failure
change_details.change_summary: PATCH 1 — SCH-04: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 added §6.1 Access-State Governance and ledger row 2.3, introducing Access-State Governance as a cross-domain arbitration primitive distinguishing authentication, entitlement, quota, model availability, platform availability, policy, security, client, organisation, and continuity/session access states. PATCH 2 — OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 added §3.7.7 Access-State Collapse and Access-State Ambiguity Failure, defining the operational failure class for systems that deny, degrade, restrict, rate-limit, interrupt, or prevent access without preserving or disclosing the operative access-controlling condition, with cross-reference to CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 §6.1. PATCH 3 — FAILURE FAMILY EXTENSION: OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 added optional controlled subtype FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY and updated controlled governance-failure language to recognise FF.GOVERNANCE_OVER_EXTENSION and FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY. PATCH 4 — OPERATIONS LEDGER UPDATE: OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 added ledger entry 1.12 documenting §3.7.7, FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY, and the §6.1 cross-reference.
change_details.doctrine_change: Caelestis doctrine and Operations taxonomy repairs already implemented outside this VIGIL pass; VIGIL records the repair path and does not modify the CAM instruments.
future_doctrine_work: No full domain rewrite is required at this stage.
Create later cross-references to IDENTITY, SECURITY, ECONOMICS, CONTINUITY, OBSERVABILITY, or other affected domains only if needed.
Continue monitoring FM-0008 evidence for recurrence and for whether user-facing access-state disclosure remains ambiguous in practice.

implementation_verification.verification_method: Record completed Caelestis repair actions in VIGIL PATCH-0005, link FM-0008 to the patch record, run VIGIL validator, and rebuild generated VIGIL indexes.
evidence: VIGIL-2026-FM-0008 exists and validates after Multi Vendor normalisation.
VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0005 records the completed SCH-04 Access-State Governance repair.
VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0005 records the completed OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Access-State Collapse / Access-State Ambiguity Failure taxonomy repair.
VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0005 records optional subtype FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY and Operations ledger entry 1.12.
FM-0008 links PATCH-0005 as its repair record.

impact_summary: PATCH-0005 preserves the VIGIL-to-Caelestis repair chain for FM-0008. The completed repair defines Access-State Governance as a cross-domain arbitration primitive and classifies Access-State Collapse / Access-State Ambiguity Failure as the operational failure class for ambiguous access-denial, degradation, restriction, rate-limit, interruption, or access-prevention conditions. The patch deliberately avoids sprawl into IDENTITY, SECURITY, ECONOMICS, CONTINUITY, OBSERVABILITY, or other domains; those domains remain affected cross-reference candidates only.
remaining_work: Continue evidence monitoring and create later cross-references only if needed. No full domain rewrite is recorded or required by this VIGIL patch note.

changed_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 — Annex B: Arbitration Layer & Resolution Model
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
cam_internal.governance_layer: access-state governance / cross-domain arbitration / runtime and governance failure taxonomy / user-facing access-state disclosure / VIGIL failure-mode-to-patch interface
cam_internal.proposal_needed: no — repairs already implemented in Caelestis
cam_internal.patch_note_needed: no — this record is the VIGIL patch note
routing_note: This VIGIL patch note records completed Caelestis repairs for FM-0008 and does not modify the CAM instruments in this repository pass.

Annex B creates the doctrine: Access-State Governance is a cross-domain arbitration primitive and access is controlled by multiple separable states that should not be collapsed into one ambiguous user-facing failure.
Operations creates the taxonomy: Access-State Collapse / Access-State Ambiguity Failure is the operational failure class used to classify incidents like FM-0008.
Avoid patch sprawl into IDENTITY, SECURITY, ECONOMICS, CONTINUITY, OBSERVABILITY, or other domain instruments at this stage; treat those domains as affected cross-reference candidates only.
review_targets: IDENTITY domain instruments — cross-reference candidate only

CONTINUITY domain instruments — cross-reference candidate only
OBSERVABILITY domain instruments — cross-reference candidate only
